title: zanthoxylum fagara subsp. fagara
intro: Zanthoxylum fagara subsp. fagara, also known as wild prickly ash, is a plant species belonging to the rutaceae family, characterized by its thorny branches and aromatic leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum fagara subsp. fagara include relief from pain and fever, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
